definition: Transcript reviewers judged Agent_1 to have a positional/process advantage, not just a numerical win.
Among process-advantage cases, Agent_1 used explicit opening anchor language in the public discussion.
Among process-advantage cases, Agent_1 submitted a formal proposal that gave Agent_1 above-proposal-mean utility.

theme: Final allocation matched Agent_1 formal proposal
definition: Among process-advantage cases, the accepted final allocation matched at least one Agent_1 formal proposal.
Among process-advantage cases, Agent_1 received at least one top-valued item.

theme: Last agent missed a top item
definition: Among process-advantage cases, the last-position agent did not receive any top-valued item.

theme: Agent_1 top item while last missed top item
definition: Among process-advantage cases, Agent_1 received a top item and the last agent did not.
Among process-advantage cases, public/proposal text used quick-resolution and efficiency/top-item framing.
At competition_level=0.0, Agent_1 did not beat the last position.

theme: All agents got top items in non-first-win cases
definition: Among ties/counterexamples, all agents received at least one top-valued item.

theme: Last agent top item in counterexamples
definition: Among first-loses cases, the last-position agent received at least one top-valued item.

theme: Agent_1 top item still not enough
definition: Among first-loses cases, Agent_1 still received a top item, so the counterexample is not just failure to secure a priority.
In N=5 runs, final allocation assigned exactly one item to each agent.
